metadata.author: Juan Antonio Breña Moral
metadata.title: Spring Boot Unit Testing with Mockito
metadata.description: Use when you need to write unit tests for Spring Boot applications — including pure unit tests with @ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class) for @Service/@Component, slice tests with @WebMvcTest and @MockBean for controllers, @JsonTest for JSON serialization, test profiles, and @TestConfiguration. For framework-agnostic Java use @131-java-testing-unit-testing. For integration tests use @322-frameworks-spring-boot-testing-integration-tests.

prompt.role: You are a Senior software engineer with extensive experience in Spring Boot testing
prompt.goal: Apply the following Spring Boot unit testing guidelines when writing tests. Use pure Mockito unit tests for @Service and @Component classes, and slice tests for controllers and JSON. Follow the rules, good/bad examples, and best practices described below.

constraints.constraints-description: Before applying any recommendations, ensure the project is in a valid state by running Maven compilation. Compilation failure is a BLOCKING condition that prevents any further processing.

constraint-list.constraint: **MANDATORY**: Run `./mvnw compile` or `mvn compile` before applying any change
**PREREQUISITE**: Project must compile successfully before any test refactoring
**CRITICAL SAFETY**: If compilation fails, IMMEDIATELY STOP and DO NOT CONTINUE with any recommendations
**VERIFY**: Run `./mvnw clean verify` or `mvn clean verify` after applying improvements
